verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70/HSP72) is a canonical cytosolic chaperone with no TM domain or GPI anchor. However, multiple published studies document its translocation to the outer face of the plasma membrane in tumor cells and under stress conditions, where it inserts into lipid rafts via affinity for negatively charged phospholipids (phosphatidylserine) exposed on the outer leaflet of cancer cells. Surface-expressed HSPA1A is recognized by NK cells and is being explored as a therapeutic target. This is a well-characterised cancer-cell/stress-induced ecto-chaperone phenomenon qualifying as cell_state_induced. The surface pool is absent (or minimal) in normal non-stressed cells, making 'yes' inappropriate, but 'contextual' is well-supported.
